vulnerabilities.title: Unbounded Input via gets()
vulnerabilities.description: `gets()` reads from stdin with no length limit and will overflow the destination buffer if input exceeds its size. This function is so dangerous it was removed from the C11 standard. Replace with `fgets(buf, sizeof(buf), stdin)`.

vulnerabilities.title: Buffer Overflow via strcpy()
vulnerabilities.description: `strcpy()` copies data into a destination buffer without checking its size. If the source string exceeds the destination buffer, memory beyond the buffer will be overwritten, potentially allowing arbitrary code execution (CWE-120). Replace with `strncpy()` or `strlcpy()` with explicit size limits.

vulnerabilities.title: Buffer Overflow via strcpy()
vulnerabilities.description: `strcpy()` copies data into a destination buffer without checking its size. If the source string exceeds the destination buffer, memory beyond the buffer will be overwritten, potentially allowing arbitrary code execution (CWE-120). Replace with `strncpy()` or `strlcpy()` with explicit size limits.

vulnerabilities.title: Potential Use-After-Free
vulnerabilities.description: Memory allocated at line 34 is freed at line 37, but the pointer may be used again at line 38. Accessing freed memory causes undefined behavior and can be exploited for arbitrary code execution (CWE-416). Set pointer to NULL immediately after free().

vulnerabilities.title: Format String Injection via printf()
vulnerabilities.description: `printf()` is called with a non-literal first argument. If this value originates from user input, an attacker can inject format specifiers (%n, %x) to read/write arbitrary memory locations (CWE-134). Always use `printf("%s", user_input)` with a literal format string.

vulnerabilities.title: Buffer Overflow via sprintf()
vulnerabilities.description: `sprintf()` writes formatted output into a buffer without checking its size. A format string that expands larger than the buffer causes overflow. Replace with `snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf), ...)` to enforce bounds.

vulnerabilities.title: Format String Injection via sprintf()
vulnerabilities.description: `sprintf()` is called with a non-literal first argument. If this value originates from user input, an attacker can inject format specifiers (%n, %x) to read/write arbitrary memory locations (CWE-134). Always use `sprintf("%s", user_input)` with a literal format string.

vulnerabilities.title: Buffer Overflow via sprintf()
vulnerabilities.description: `sprintf()` writes formatted output into a buffer without checking its size. A format string that expands larger than the buffer causes overflow. Replace with `snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf), ...)` to enforce bounds.

vulnerabilities.title: Format String Injection via sprintf()
vulnerabilities.description: `sprintf()` is called with a non-literal first argument. If this value originates from user input, an attacker can inject format specifiers (%n, %x) to read/write arbitrary memory locations (CWE-134). Always use `sprintf("%s", user_input)` with a literal format string.

vulnerabilities.title: Possible Out-of-Bounds Write via memcpy()
vulnerabilities.description: `memcpy()` is called without a size derived from `sizeof(destination)`. If the copy length exceeds the destination buffer, adjacent memory is overwritten (CWE-125). Always use `sizeof(dest)` or `dest_len` as the size argument.

vulnerabilities.title: Double-Free Detected
vulnerabilities.description: The same pointer appears to be freed more than once (lines 43, 44). Double-free corrupts the heap allocator and can be exploited to gain code execution (CWE-415). Set pointer to NULL after first free to prevent this.

vulnerabilities.title: Command Injection via system()
vulnerabilities.description: `system()` passes a string to the OS shell for execution. If any part of this string is user-controlled, an attacker can inject shell metacharacters (`;`, `|`, `&&`) to execute arbitrary commands (CWE-78). Replace with `execve()` with argument arrays — never pass user input to `system()`.

patch_results.description: Replace sprintf with snprintf to enforce buffer limit
Add literal format string to printf-family calls (already applied — duplicate finding)
Guard free() with NULL check and zero pointer after
Set pointer to NULL immediately after free()
Replace strcpy with strncpy + explicit null-terminator
Replace shell-based execution with direct arg list
Replace sprintf with snprintf to enforce buffer limit
Add literal format string to printf-family calls (already applied — duplicate finding)
Add literal format string to printf-family calls
Replace unsafe input function with safe bounded alternative
Replace strcpy with strncpy + explicit null-terminator
